name: IntunePrepTool
description: A PowerShell GUI tool for streamlining application packaging for Microsoft Intune. Supports MSI, MSIX, EXE, PowerShell scripts, Winget, Chocolatey, and scheduled tasks. Features automatic script generation, direct Intune upload, registry modifications, shortcuts, and AI-assisted configuration via Azure OpenAI GPT-4.
keywords: how to package apps for intune
author: Rink Turksma

securityCheck.aiSummary: The IntunePrepTool uses Microsoft Graph to register apps and assign permissions, which is legitimate for Intune/Graph workflows. However, there are notable security concerns: (1) remote code execution risk from downloading and installing Winget via MicrosoftDesktopApp.ps1; (2) plaintext storage of secrets in addAppRegPlainText.ps1; and (3) local credential storage/handling in addAppReg.ps1 that could be exposed if the host is compromised. Mitigations include validating and signing downloaded content, eliminating plaintext secret exports, and adopting centralized secret management with least-privilege access.
